name: LanceDB
description: LanceDB is a developer-friendly, open-source embedded vector database for multimodal AI. Built on the Lance columnar format (Arrow-native), it stores raw data, embeddings, and metadata in a single system. LanceDB supports zero-copy reads, fast vector search with IVF-PQ and HNSW indexes, and scales from local development to cloud object storage with separation of storage and compute.
core_strengths: Arrow-native vector search with zero-copy reads
Multimodal data — text, images, embeddings in one table
Separation of storage and compute for cloud scale
Fast approximate nearest neighbor search (IVF-PQ, HNSW)
SQL-like filtering with metadata alongside vectors
best_use_cases: Multimodal AI applications with combined text/image search
Local-first vector search for desktop and edge apps
ML model training data storage with embeddings
Semantic search over documents with metadata filtering
remarks: LanceDB v1 uses a multi-file on-disk format (not truly single-file). For single-file portability, use LanceDB v0.x. Vector index creation is a separate explicit step; queries without an index perform brute-force search. Cloud object storage support requires additional configuration for S3/GCS/Azure.
